---
title: "CDADE Literature Review"
author: "CDADE Team"
date: "2026-07-05"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

# Literature Review

## Motivation

CDADE targets hierarchical anomaly detection for count time series. The project extends the static top-$k$ ensemble of Eze et al. (2023) by introducing a coherence-preserving reconciliation layer and a drift-aware dynamic selection stage.

## Literature framing

- Eze et al. (2023) motivate the use of a detector ensemble for public-health surveillance, but the original approach is static and does not explicitly enforce hierarchical coherence.
- Hierarchical reconciliation follows the MinT perspective, where forecasts are reconciled through a summing matrix so that aggregate and leaf-level predictions remain additive.
- Dynamic ensemble selection is motivated by META-DES-style competence estimation and diversity weighting, with drift detection used to reset the active pool when the recent regime changes.
- The evaluation protocol follows the public-health anomaly-detection setting and emphasizes precision/recall, AUC-PR, and NAB rather than a single point-adjusted F1 metric.

## How the current report connects to the README

The report intentionally reflects the README narrative: a hybrid L1/L2/L3 system, baselines against which CDADE is compared, and a rigorous evaluation loop that is intended to support later multi-dataset claims.
